notes.anchor: If you and your family are well, it is well; I too am well
notes.note: The conventional opening health-formula of an official letter (Latin S.V.B.E.E.V.); the variant here adds the recipient's household.
"The Antonii" are the two brothers acting together — Marcus Antonius and Lucius Antonius — whose combined forces had entered Lepidus's province of Narbonese Gaul.
A waystation on the Aurelian road in Narbonese Gaul, near modern Le Cannet/Fréjus; Lepidus's camp there on the river Argenteus marked the front line against Antony in late May 43 BC.

notes.anchor: Silanus and Culleo have left him
notes.note: Two of Lepidus's own officers (probably M. Iunius Silanus and Q. Terentius Culleo) had defected to Antony against his wishes and then returned; Lepidus spares them but pointedly gives them no command.

notes.anchor: we shall not fail the Senate or the commonwealth
notes.note: A protestation of loyalty that proved hollow within days: by 29 May Lepidus had joined his army to Antony's, an act for which the Senate declared him a public enemy.
